brokers.name: Pepperstone
brokers.notes: FCA (FRN 684312) and ASIC (AFSL 414530) confirmed as current regulators; also licensed via CySEC, DFSA, BaFin, CMA Kenya, and SCB Bahamas entities (individual license numbers for these not independently cross-checked on each regulator's own register — confirmed only via Pepperstone's own site). No regulatory fines or sanctions found in this pass. Recurring complaint themes: withdrawal delays and spread widening during high volatility — not unusual for the sector, no rebrand/exit-scam pattern found. Note: a lookalike domain 'pepperstone.finance' runs its own Trustpilot page — not affiliated with the real Pepperstone. Min deposit is officially $0 (aggregator sources cite ~$200 as a practical starting point). GER40 spread on the Standard account and the exact scalping/news/weekend policy wording are sourced from third-party aggregators, not a Pepperstone-owned page directly — worth a live re-check before treating as exact. Affiliate application pending as of 2026-07-11 — do not add an affiliate link until approved.
brokers.leverage: Up to 1:30 retail (FCA/ASIC/CySEC entities), 1:1000 professional/offshore entities

externalReview.note: Rating/count reconstructed from search snippets — Trustpilot blocked direct automated fetch (403); confirm the live count before quoting it precisely.

secondaryReview.note: Direct fetch also blocked (403); figure from search snippets.

brokers.name: IC Markets
brokers.notes: IMPORTANT: IC Markets is rebranding to 'IC' — the domain migration from icmarkets.com to ic.com is happening on 2026-07-11 (confirmed live on-page: a scheduled migration window that day). Both icmarkets.com and ic.com currently resolve to the same content in parallel — worth a follow-up check in a few days to confirm ic.com is the stable, permanent canonical domain before switching the affiliate URL over. CySEC fined the EU entity twice: €200,000 (for routing EU retail clients to an offshore entity to offer up to 1000:1 leverage, circumventing the 30:1 retail cap) and a further €50,000 (Jul 2024, best-execution/cost-disclosure failures) — both reportedly under appeal. FCA temporary UK permission was cancelled Dec 2023 — not currently authorized to do regulated business in the UK despite historic name recognition there. ASIC (AFSL 335692) and CySEC (362/18, confirmed directly on CySEC's own register) are the two solidly confirmed regulators; FSCA South Africa and SCB Bahamas status is disputed by third-party sources. GER40 spread (~1.3 pts, both account types) and EURUSD spread figures are broker-marketing averages with some inconsistency versus third-party test data — treat as indicative, not exact.
brokers.leverage: Up to 1:30 retail (CySEC/EU), 1:500 professional (EU), 1:1000 offshore (Seychelles) entities

externalReview.note: Via search snippets, not a live fetch (403). Domain migrating to ic.com on 2026-07-11 — re-check the URL after that date.

secondaryReview.note: FPA shows a qualitative ~80.6% positive figure, not a clean 0-5 rating — not converted to a star score to avoid a misleading number.

brokers.name: FP Markets
brokers.notes: ASIC (AFSL 286354) confirmed; CySEC licence 371/18 confirmed directly on CySEC's own register. Regulatory history: a 2014 ASIC court-enforceable undertaking over compliance-process gaps (resolved, historical) and — more recently — a €100,000 CySEC settlement (Feb 2026) over a possible MiFIR Art. 42(5) marketing-restriction breach spanning March 2022–Aug 2025. A third-party compliance-watchdog source (FinTelegram, unconfirmed elsewhere) alleges an offshore SVG entity lets EEA clients bypass leverage caps and KYC timing — not independently verified. GER40 spread not found in any source checked — the figures below are estimates pending a live quote. Min deposit varies $50–100 across sources; used $100. ForexPeaceArmy rating could not be reliably determined (conflicting data — one source cites FPA's 0-star policy flag, another cites 3.1/5), so no secondary review score is shown.

externalReview.note: Review count is imprecise — sources ranged 6,000–10,000; blocked from a direct live fetch (403).

brokers.name: Global Prime
brokers.notes: Regulated by ASIC (AFSL 385620) via FMGP Trading Group Pty Ltd — the same licensed entity that also trades as Fusion Markets (not just a loosely-related 'sister company'; same AFSL number, same ABN 74 146 086 017). No ASIC enforcement action found. GER40 spread not published by Global Prime directly — the figures below are estimates pending a live check. News-trading and weekend-holding policy are not explicitly stated anywhere found; EA/algo and scalping are explicitly allowed per Global Prime's own account-types page. cTrader/TradingView availability is unclear — the official FAQ suggests 'coming soon' but some external references suggest it may already be live.
brokers.leverage: Up to 1:30 retail (AU), 1:500 professional/wholesale (tier breakdown not independently confirmed)

externalReview.note: Blocked from direct live fetch (403); figure from search snippets, ranged 385-480 across sources.

secondaryReview.note: Blocked from direct live fetch (403); figure from search snippets.

brokers.name: Fusion Markets
brokers.notes: Regulated by ASIC (AFSL 385620) — the same licensed entity (FMGP Trading Group Pty Ltd) that also trades as Global Prime; both brands reportedly share the same liquidity provider (PrimeXM). No ASIC enforcement action found. Alberta Securities Commission (Canada) lists Fusion on its Investment Caution List — a standard 'not registered in this province' notice, not a misconduct finding; applies to many offshore-facing brokers. A clone site impersonating the Fusion name was separately flagged by the UK FCA — unrelated to the real fusionmarkets.com. GER40 spread not reliably found (third-party figures were contradictory) — the figures below are rough estimates pending a live check. EA/scalping/news/weekend-holding policy wording is sourced from aggregators, not Fusion's own Product Disclosure Statement.

externalReview.note: Blocked from direct live fetch (403); figure from a secondary aggregator citing a June 2026 snapshot.

brokers.name: Admirals
brokers.notes: IMPORTANT CORRECTIONS vs. older/third-party listings: Admirals sold its Australian (ASIC) entity to PU Prime in Dec 2024/Jan 2025 — no longer ASIC-regulated. Estonia's Finantsinspektsioon revoked the Estonian (EFSA) investment-firm license in April 2026 at Admirals' own request, consolidating EU operations into the Cyprus entity (Admirals Europe Ltd, CySEC 201/13, confirmed directly on CySEC's own register) — group HQ remains in Tallinn but the licensed EU entity is now Cyprus-based. Also regulated via FCA (UK, FRN 595450), JSC (Jordan), FSCA (South Africa), CMA (Kenya), and FSA (Seychelles). Recurring withdrawal complaints found in reviews (one citing ~$9,021 withheld); no rebrand/exit-scam pattern — both the Australia sale and Estonia license surrender are disclosed, above-board restructuring. PayPal is explicitly not supported. GER40 spread and exact scalping/news-trading policy wording were not found on a primary Admirals page (contract-specs page is JS-rendered, blocked automated fetch) — treat as an estimate. ForexPeaceArmy rating could not be reliably determined, so no secondary review score is shown. CORRECTION (2026-07-11): a first-pass claim that TradingView is available was wrong — a deeper check of Admirals' own platforms page (via Wayback Machine, since admirals.com/admiralmarkets.com block live automated fetch) found no TradingView, cTrader, Iress, or third-party copy-trading integration at all; only MT4, MT5, MetaTrader WebTrader/Supreme Edition, Admirals' own 'native' web platform, and a mobile app.

externalReview.note: Blocked from direct live fetch (403); a separate unclaimed/unmanaged Trustpilot listing under a slightly different name also exists — don't conflate the two.

brokers.name: Vantage
brokers.notes: IMPORTANT — do not publish 'FCA-regulated' as a plain claim: the FCA issued a warning dated 2026-04-02 against 'vantagemarkets.com' stating it 'is not authorised by us and may be targeting people in the UK' and 'is not associated with the FCA authorised firm Vantage Global Prime LLP' (FRN 590299) — it's unresolved whether this is a clone-domain issue or the FCA flagging that the global/offshore brand shouldn't solicit UK clients under that separate FCA-authorised entity's name. Regulation list below deliberately excludes FCA pending manual resolution of this warning; only the entity's ASIC/FSCA/CIMA/VFSC licenses are shown. ForexPeaceArmy reported (Dec 2024) a Vantage employee posting fake independent client endorsements — a real credibility issue distinct from ordinary broker complaints, weighed into the trust score below. Large Trustpilot (4.3, ~13,400 reviews) vs ForexPeaceArmy (1.95, ~262 reviews) gap — typical pattern for this broker category (Trustpilot skews toward onboarding satisfaction, FPA toward escalated disputes) but the gap is wider than most brokers here. Rebranded from 'Vantage FX' to 'Vantage' in Nov 2021 — a disclosed marketing rebrand tied to multi-asset expansion, not a rebrand-to-evade pattern. GER40 spread and scalping/news-trading policy are sourced from secondary aggregators, not a Vantage-owned page — treat as estimates pending a live check.
brokers.leverage: Up to 1:30 retail (ASIC entity), 1:500 offshore (CIMA/VFSC entities) — some sources cite higher tiers, unconfirmed

externalReview.note: Vantage's own press materials cite a lower, older count (~9,000) — use the live figure, not the press release.

secondaryReview.note: Notably low vs Trustpilot; includes a Dec 2024 report of a Vantage employee posting fake client endorsements.

brokers.name: Eightcap
brokers.notes: Regulated by ASIC (AFSL 391441), FCA (FRN 921296), and CySEC (246/14) as primary tier-1 entities, plus offshore entities in the Bahamas, Seychelles, and Mauritius. No regulatory fines or sanctions found. FCA issued a clone-firm warning against 'Eightcap VIP / eightcap.vip' impersonating the real, FCA-authorised Eightcap Group Ltd — unrelated to the genuine broker, just a naming collision to watch for. Trustpilot (~4.0/5, ~3,750 reviews) vs ForexPeaceArmy (~2.07/5, ~48 reviews) diverge sharply — same pattern seen across several brokers here (Trustpilot skews positive onboarding, FPA skews disputes), not treated as a unique red flag. Also offers TradeLocker alongside MT4/MT5/TradingView (no cTrader) — TradeLocker isn't in this tool's platform filter/schema, mentioned here for completeness. GER40 spread and exact leverage/deposit-method details are sourced from secondary aggregators, not confirmed directly on an Eightcap-owned page — treat as estimates.
brokers.leverage: Up to 1:30 retail (ASIC/FCA/CySEC entities), 1:500 professional (tier not independently confirmed)

brokers.name: Tickmill
brokers.notes: Regulated by FCA (717270, confirmed on FCA's own register) and CySEC (278/15, confirmed on CySEC's own register), plus FSCA South Africa, FSA Seychelles, and Labuan FSA (Malaysia) entities — the Labuan entity (Tickmill Asia Ltd) was initially flagged as unconfirmed (found only in a client-agreement PDF), then directly confirmed via Tickmill's own official Affiliate Brand Guidelines document (Aug 2025), which lists all five regulated entities explicitly. No confirmed regulatory fines or sanctions found in the 2024-2026 window. An older (~2020) Malaysia Securities Commission Investor Alert List listing could not be found on the SC's current live list — treat as resolved/historical, not a current flag, though this wasn't exhaustively confirmed. Scalping and hedging are explicitly permitted per Tickmill's own FAQ ('no time limitations for keeping positions open') — better-documented than most brokers here. Segregated funds and investor compensation confirmed directly (FSCS up to £85,000 for UK clients, ICF up to €20,000 for EU clients) — note a search result surfaced a false '£120,000 FSCS, per the Bank of England' claim that does not check out (the Bank of England doesn't set FSCS limits) and was deliberately excluded. GER40 spread and exact Raw-account commission ($2 vs $3/side, sources disagree) are estimates pending a live check. Affiliate application pending as of 2026-07-11 — do not add an affiliate link until approved.

externalReview.note: Blocked from direct live fetch (403); figure corroborated by secondary sources only.
